question: I have sciatica on the left side. I have pain when walking or standing for a long period of time.
context_question: Patient has left-sided sciatica with pain when walking or standing for years. Physical therapy and weight loss helped, but pain persists after walking over a mile.
answer: MRI shows degenerative changes in lumbar spine, acute edema at L5-S1, and disc bulges causing neural foraminal compromise. Severe pain may require surgical treatment if daily activities are limited.

question: I am a 33-year-old female. I recently had a chest X-ray done, and there were opacities in my right infraclavicular region.
context_question: Patient is a 33-year-old female with opacities in the right infraclavicular region noted on a recent chest X-ray. She seeks clarification on a follow-up X-ray.
answer: Chest X-ray shows suspicious opacities in the right infraclavicular region, which may indicate lung pathology or normal structures. Further imaging may be needed for clarification.

question: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the right ankle was performed following a climbing fall from 3.89 yards, with inversion rolling on impact. This occurred a year ago, and I have had on
context_question: Patient had an MRI of the right ankle after a climbing fall a year ago, experiencing ongoing pain around the anterior talo-fibular ligament and occasional swelling.
answer: MRI confirms a sprain of the ATFL and CFL, with significant effusion in the ankle joint. No tear is observed, and the TFCC shows mild sprain but is intact.

question: I am having a lump around three to four centimeters in diameter on my right buttock for the past many years. It pains a little when it is pressed.
context_question: Patient has a 3-4 cm lump on the right buttock for years, which hurts when pressed. The lump has slightly increased in size recently.
answer: Ultrasound suggests a slow flow venous malformation, a benign condition. A new ultrasound is recommended to assess current size and features.

question: I would like to get a an opinion regarding the attached chest radiograph .Â I wish to know if there are any abnormalities like scarring.
context_question: Patient seeks an opinion on a chest radiograph to check for abnormalities or scarring.
answer: The chest X-ray is normal except for slightly prominent lung markings. No scarring or fluid collection is observed.

question: I have been suffering from back pain (right side only) for two years, and this hurts a lot when sitting or laying down for a while (like 20 seconds). So I got an electromyography test that
context_question: Patient has right-sided back pain for two years, worsening when sitting or lying down. An electromyography test was performed.
answer: X-ray and MRI findings indicate an old fracture at L4, left-sided nerve root compressions due to disc herniations, and facet arthropathy at multiple levels.

question: I suffer from back pain. Please give me a second opinion.
context_question: Patient seeks a second opinion regarding back pain and has attached reports.
answer: Reports show small multilevel osteophytes, desiccated discs at L3-4 and L4-5, and mild disc bulges without nerve root compression. Nature of pain needs clarification.

question: I need your expert opinion on something really concerning. I am 47 years old and have previously recovered from pneumococcal disease, but for the last few months, I have been struggling wit
context_question: Patient, 47 years old, is experiencing worsening shortness of breath after recovering from pneumococcal disease. A CT scan confirmed abnormal patterns linked to previous infections.
answer: Shortness of breath may indicate various issues. CT scan report is unclear; further details are needed. Pneumonia, if treated properly, should not lead to complications.

question: I had a CT scan with contrast (upper abdomen, lungs, head, and neck) due to multiple enlarged right cervical lymph nodes. On biopsy, I was diagnosed with Kikuchi lymphadenitis.
context_question: Patient had a CT scan due to enlarged right cervical lymph nodes and was diagnosed with Kikuchi lymphadenitis. There is bulging in the left TMJ and masseter muscle.
answer: No mass or inflammation in the left parotid region is observed. The left masseter is bulging, likely due to temporomandibular disorder (TMD).

question: Could you provide a second opinion on a radiology report? This is for my friend with right-sided rib fractures (4th and 5th) who exhibits the following symptoms: pain in the right side o
context_question: Patient seeks a second opinion on a report for a friend with right-sided rib fractures and symptoms of chest pain and shoulder pain.
answer: Rib fractures are old and healed. No pleural reaction or thickening is observed. Symptoms may originate from the cervical spine or spinal cord.

sympt: pain in the right side of the chest

question: Recently, I had to have a CT scan on my neck vertebrae following an accident. Thankfully, the scan came back as normal; however, I have a question regarding the accuracy of CT scans as a
context_question: Patient had a CT scan on neck vertebrae after an accident, which was normal. She questions the accuracy of CT scans for diagnosing bones.
answer: CT scans are excellent for detecting bone fractures and abnormalities. Your normal scan effectively ruled out major issues post-accident.

question: I am experiencing right flank pain and have undergone both a CT scan and an MRI. Please interpret the findings from these examinations.
context_question: Patient has right flank pain and has undergone both a CT scan and an MRI, seeking interpretation of the findings.
answer: CT shows an extrarenal pelvis in the right kidney with mild fullness, but no calculus or mass. Other abdominal structures appear normal.

question: Why does my low back pain and hip and pelvic pain increase suddenly?
context_question: Patient inquires about sudden increases in low back, hip, and pelvic pain.
answer: MRI shows fluid collection in the uterine cavity and a Tarlov cyst at S2, which may cause pain by compressing the nerve root.

question: It has been three weeks since falling from a height on a straightened dorsally bent palm. Considerable swelling and hematoma appeared near the anatomical snuffbox and on the elbow.
context_question: Patient fell from a height three weeks ago, experiencing swelling and hematoma near the anatomical snuffbox and elbow. A scaphoid fracture was diagnosed.
answer: Reports show no scaphoid fracture, but soft tissue contusion and mild sprain of the TFCC. Surgery is not required; clinical evaluation is needed.

question: Recently, I got a fracture in my sacrum. After taking a rest for two months, the doctor advised me to do an MRI scan.
context_question: Patient had a sacral fracture and underwent an MRI after two months of rest. Mild pain and numbness persist after exercise.
answer: MRI shows no fractures, only callus formation indicating healing. Avoid heavy lifting and sports, and continue prescribed medications.

question: I am suffering from poor balance, difficulty in swallowing, tingling in the fourth and fifth fingers of both hands, difficulty in standing for more than three minutes, slow heart rate, a
context_question: Patient reports poor balance, swallowing difficulty, tingling in fingers, and dizziness, with symptoms starting suddenly nine years ago.
answer: MRI findings show a bony hemangioma in the spine and signs of benign intracranial hypertension. No significant abnormalities are observed.

question: I have a few lumps (my doctor suggested as lipoma) underneath my left breast. They vary in size.
context_question: Patient has lumps under left breast for years, with mild right pelvic pain and recent CT scan showing lesions.
answer: CT scan shows benign lesions on left iliac and right femoral areas. Follow-up imaging in a year is suggested. The femoral lesion may represent osteoid osteoma, possibly causing right-sided pain.

question: I am a 22-year-old non-smoker, and I have been through a huge number of tests trying to figure out what is wrong. My biggest fear is lung cancer, specifically bronchoalveolar carcinoma.
context_question: 22-year-old non-smoker fears lung cancer, has shortness of breath, cough with phlegm, finger clubbing, and lymph nodes.
answer: CT scan shows ground glass opacity, which is nonspecific and does not indicate bronchoalveolar carcinoma. Follow-up imaging is needed to monitor changes.

question: I have been sick for two years. I was diagnosed with human papilloma virus (HPV) two years back, but no treatment was done.
context_question: Patient diagnosed with HPV two years ago, now has anemia, lower back pain, fatigue, and pelvic pain.
answer: Ultrasound shows normal uterus and ovaries. Endometrial thickness is at the upper limit of normal. Discuss findings with your doctor.

question: During a ski trip a few months ago, I fell on my outstretched hand during a jump and injured my shoulder. I did a magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scan immediately after, and the radiolo
context_question: Patient injured shoulder during a ski trip, MRI scan shows no major issues, but shoulder pain persists.
answer: MRI shows bony contusion at the greater tubercle of the humerus. This may require medication and therapy due to prolonged pain.

question: I am a 70 year old female. My height is 5'5' and weight is 155 lbs.
context_question: 70-year-old female with discomfort in right buttock for one year, recent nuclear scan done.
answer: Nuclear scan shows no infection around the prosthesis but suspected infection in the bowel or fallopian tube on the right side.

question: I have had left hip pain for two years. I had hip pain following a bicycle ride and was admitted to the hospital for severe lower back pain on the left side.
context_question: Patient has left hip pain for two years, treated with muscle relaxants and physical therapy.
answer: MRI shows normal hip structures but suspicious inflammation of the gluteal tendon attachment may contribute to pain.

question: I have been experiencing upper glute pain on the right side for two years, which hurts while sitting or lying down facing upwards. I had an magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scan done in
context_question: Patient has upper glute pain on the right side for two years, MRI scan done previously.
answer: MRI shows old fracture at right inferior articular process of L4 and nerve root compressions due to disc herniation.

question: I had done the whole body MRI scan six months ago. Now due to arising some neurological symptoms neurologist advised an MRI brain scan with contrast.
context_question: Patient had whole body MRI, now advised MRI brain scan due to neurological symptoms.
answer: Whole-body MRI is general and may miss detailed brain assessment, hence a new contrast MRI brain is recommended.

question: I have been suffering from back pain (right side) for a few years now, and this hurts a lot while sitting and lying down. I got an electromyography test showing mild chronic dysfunction on
context_question: Patient has right side back pain for years, with electromyography test showing mild chronic dysfunction.
answer: CT and MRI show disc desiccation, mild posterior disc bulge, and neural foraminal compromise at L4-L5 levels.

question: I am 27 years old, and I have been dealing with a lump under my left rib cage that is visible. Alongside this, I have been experiencing constant nausea, dizziness, and stomach pain for s
context_question: 27-year-old with visible lump under left rib cage, constant nausea, dizziness, and stomach pain, with history of gastritis.
answer: Ultrasound shows normal results. A CT scan is suggested to further characterize the lump under the left rib cage.

question: I had a computed tomography (CT) scan done for suspected lymphoma because of high C reactive protein (CRP), erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR), white blood cells (WBC), night sweats, a
context_question: Patient had CT scan for suspected lymphoma due to high CRP, ESR, WBC, night sweats, and fever.
answer: Ultrasound shows a hypoechoic nodule likely an enlarged cervical lymph node. No vascularity noted. Other neck structures appear normal.

question: I need a detailed magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) review and report for my problem. Is that possible?
context_question: Patient requests a detailed MRI review and report.
answer: You can upload your scan with a proper history and areas of concern for a detailed review.

question: My doctor recommended I get an magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the prostate and kidney. I have visible blood in the urine after exercise.
context_question: Patient advised MRI of prostate and kidney due to visible blood in urine after exercise.
answer: MRI is a noninvasive technique for assessing prostate conditions. Gadolinium contrast is optional. PSA levels and urine infection tests are also recommended.

question: I have been diagnosed with osteoarthritis in both hands. One doctor suggested erosions in the left hand while another read that the right hand did not see evidence of that.
context_question: Patient diagnosed with osteoarthritis, questioning the presence of erosions in hands.
answer: Osteoarthritis is not autoimmune. X-ray shows joint narrowing and sclerosis in the left hand. Treatment includes anti-inflammatory medications and therapy.

question: I am looking for a detailed read and interpretation of the MRA. Please be as detailed as possible with any abnormal findings you may see.
context_question: Patient requests detailed interpretation of MRA due to progressive vision loss and headaches.
answer: MRA shows normal internal carotid arteries, hypoplasia of right anterior cerebral artery, and no vascular malformations. No aneurysm or dissection noted.

question: I underwent quadriceps tendon repair surgery last year. After that, as per the magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) finding, I developed patchy marrow edema in the patella and lateral femora
context_question: I developed patchy marrow edema in the patella and lateral femoral condyle after quadriceps tendon repair surgery last year.
answer: MRI shows thinning and irregularity of the patellar articular cartilage, suggestive of chondromalacia patellae. Measurements in DICOM format are needed for further assessment.

question: I want to know about the vertebral artery dissection. I had a traumatic brain injury (TBI) (with neck trauma too), no loss of consciousness, no amnesia.
context_question: I had a traumatic brain injury (TBI) with neck trauma and want to know about vertebral artery dissection.
answer: The T2 hyperintensity in the right lateral medulla may indicate mild edema if there is no restricted diffusion. You might have a non-dominant right vertebral artery.

question: I have been experiencing persistent loss of balance for the past nine months. Please check my contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) brain scan plates, especially the cerebellum
context_question: I have been experiencing persistent loss of balance for the past nine months and want to know about my MRI brain scan.
answer: The MRI scan shows a normal cerebellum with no obvious abnormality. Enhancement in the cerebellum is normal, but a complete brain scan is necessary to determine the cause of balance loss.

question: I have a level II disc replacement involving C4 - C6 and a Mobi-C disc surgery scheduled soon. I wanted to get your professional opinion on my computed tomography (CAT) (CT) scan.
context_question: I have a level II disc replacement involving C4 - C6 and want your opinion on my CT scan.
answer: The CT shows that the prosthetic discs are properly aligned, but protruded a few millimeters forwards. Discuss with your spine surgeon whether this is a severe condition.

question: I did an ultrasound abdomen. I want to know if there is an abnormality in the gallbladder.
context_question: I want to know if there is an abnormality in the gallbladder after an ultrasound and I have had right upper quadrant pain for two months.
answer: The gallbladder appears normal based on the image, but right upper quadrant pain may indicate other pathologies. A thorough clinical examination is necessary due to ongoing symptoms.

question: I have had an abdominal ultrasound for a suspected small superficial lipoma. However, the sonographer advised that nothing showed and that the tissue was normal.
context_question: Patient had an abdominal ultrasound for a suspected small superficial lipoma; the sonographer reported normal tissue.
answer: The ultrasound report shows the epidermis and dermis as a thin, hyperechoic layer, with a normal subcutaneous layer. The report is normal. Please consult your physician.

question: Can a person take alcohol after radiotherapy?
context_question: Can a person take alcohol after radiotherapy?
answer: There is no absolute contraindication for alcohol after radiotherapy. It depends on the condition being treated. If liver capacity is compromised, alcohol may increase liver load.

question: I have nose cancer, so I am going to get radiotherapy. The doctor said that the radiation might touch the eye, which may lead to loss of vision and eye removal.
context_question: I have nose cancer and will get radiotherapy. Can I prevent loss of vision and eye removal?
answer: With conventional radiation, it is difficult to save the eye. Computerized radiation techniques like IMRT or VMAT may reduce the chance of losing the eye, but certainty cannot be guaranteed.

question: I just recently had magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). I wanted to get your opinion on it.
context_question: I just had MRI and want your opinion. I am 52 years old with a family history of prostate cancer. My recent PSA score was 2.
answer: I am unable to open the files you attached. Please resend them for review.

question: Foot pain started 18 months ago after running. It has persisted, but the severity of pain depends on how active I am.
context_question: Foot pain started 18 months ago after running and has become more severe recently. Please review my X-ray.
answer: Please send me the full X-ray to review the whole joint, like the talocalcaneal joint.
